jokes.text: You see, I'm a ninth-level octopriest in the Church of the Reasonableists... where all human souls will be transmuted when Zorp the Lizard God passes through Jupiter's Sphincter.
jokes.setup: A seemingly normal religious presentation about land preservation
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd sci-fi religious terminology including 'Jupiter's Sphincter'
jokes.explanation: Classic Parks & Rec absurdist humor with escalating specificity. 'Ninth-level octopriest' builds to the ridiculous 'Jupiter's Sphincter' finale.

jokes.text: Hail Zorp
jokes.setup: The insane religious presentation just concluded
jokes.punchline: Leslie's defeated, matter-of-fact delivery of the cult greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Leslie's practical desperation leads her to go along with the cult greeting

jokes.text: So, hold onto your straws, everybody, because Mama's going grasping
jokes.setup: Leslie needs to find any reason to preserve the land
jokes.punchline: Self-aware metaphor mixing 'grasping at straws' with maternal nickname
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Leslie's self-aware desperation in character-specific language

jokes.text: In 1980, there were more than 600 million Indiana Brown Ants in that forest. Today, there are only 595 million.
jokes.setup: Urgent environmental crisis presentation
jokes.punchline: Tiny decrease over 40+ years presented as catastrophic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke about manufactured environmental urgency

jokes.text: At that rate, they'll all be gone by the year 203060.
jokes.setup: The minimal ant population decline
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous timeline of 200,000+ years in the future
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that mathematically extends the absurd timeline, very Leslie

jokes.text: I call it Disneyland.
jokes.setup: Jamm describing an amusement park concept
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have invented Disneyland
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm moment - his oblivious arrogance in claiming to have invented something famous

jokes.text: You guys, I am Roscoe. I am Vice President of Cool New Shizz here at Gryzzl.
jokes.setup: Tech company executive introduction
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous corporate title 'Vice President of Cool New Shizz'
jokes.explanation: Solid satire of tech company culture with absurd job titles

jokes.text: We had this far-out brain tornado... We should get a brity to come kick it with us small-time
jokes.setup: Tech bro explaining their strategy
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous tech-speak including 'brain tornado' and 'brity'
jokes.explanation: Good tech bro parody but somewhat predictable in its absurdity

jokes.text: That's why I refuse to write my signature in cursive.
jokes.setup: Ron preferring quality over flash
jokes.punchline: Absurd example of rejecting 'flash' by refusing cursive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - taking a reasonable principle to an absurd extreme with total conviction

jokes.text: The sales for Chick-fil-A went through the roof once Elton John bought them.
jokes.setup: Giving examples of celebrity business partnerships
jokes.punchline: Completely false claim about Elton John and Chick-fil-A
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic from Tom, playing on celebrity endorsement confusion

jokes.text: The only celebrities I recognize are furniture maker Garry Knox Bennett, designer of the notorious 16-penny nail cabinet
jokes.setup: Ron dismissing celebrity culture
jokes.punchline: His 'celebrities' are obscure craftspeople with hyper-specific credentials
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Ron's celebrities are exactly what you'd expect, with perfect specificity

jokes.text: and Magnus, the 5x5 bull elk I saw near my cabin two years ago. Took a shot at the bastard, and he stuck his antlers through the door of my meat shed.
jokes.setup: Ron naming his second celebrity
jokes.punchline: An elk he failed to hunt that destroyed his property
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron storytelling - specific hunting failure with property damage, treating an elk as a celebrity nemesis

jokes.text: One day, Magnus, I will wear you as a jacket.
jokes.setup: Ron's ongoing feud with the elk
jokes.punchline: Ominous direct threat to the elk
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff - Ron's matter-of-fact delivery of a ridiculous revenge fantasy

jokes.text: Executive. Trampoline. Computer. Trampoline.
jokes.setup: April suggesting career words, Andy responding
jokes.punchline: Andy responds 'Trampoline' to everything
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - finding one word he likes and sticking with it regardless of context

jokes.text: Well, I love it when you talk Point of Sale docs. You know it turns me on. But no.
jokes.setup: Ben asking Leslie to sign documents with Ron
jokes.punchline: Mock arousal at bureaucratic documents before firm rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - sexualizing bureaucracy before being petty

jokes.text: There's no way that I will be in the same room with Ron Swanson unless it is during a police lineup when I am pointing him out as the man who betrayed me.
jokes.setup: Leslie refusing to be in the same room as Ron
jokes.punchline: Elaborate police lineup revenge fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's dramatics and specific revenge fantasies

jokes.text: 'That's him, officer! Ronald Swanson.' 'Thank you, Leslie, that's the fifth crime you've solved this month. I'm not supposed to do this, but let me give you a badge and...'
jokes.setup: Leslie's police lineup fantasy
jokes.punchline: Fantasy escalates to her becoming a police hero
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to make everything about her civic heroism, good character work

jokes.text: I recently had a bit of a health scare, just a couple weeks in the hospital. Oh, God. This story's terrible so far.
jokes.setup: Craig starting what seems like a serious health story
jokes.punchline: Someone's immediate horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Good reaction humor - immediate dread at where Craig's story is going

jokes.text: So, I fulfilled one of my lifelong dreams, and I became a Notary Public.
jokes.setup: Craig's life-changing health scare leading to pursuing dreams
jokes.punchline: His dream was becoming a notary public
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up for something profound, delivers mundane bureaucracy

jokes.text: No, I'm not. And infinity plus one year.
jokes.setup: Ben asking if Leslie will stop acting like a little kid
jokes.punchline: Leslie responds with ultimate little kid comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - proving Ben's point while denying it with childish escalation

jokes.text: Bloosh has won the Pulitzer Prize for best top ten listicle two years in a row.
jokes.setup: Tom defending Bloosh's legitimacy
jokes.punchline: Pulitzer Prize category for listicles
jokes.explanation: Good satire of clickbait culture and award inflation

jokes.text: 'Barely a President. William Henry Harrison's Thirty-Two Days in the White House.'
jokes.setup: Bill announcing his self-published biography
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest title about Harrison's failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent title that perfectly captures Harrison's historical insignificance

jokes.text: Um, spoilers much? Now I'll never read that book.
jokes.setup: Bill revealing details about his Harrison biography
jokes.punchline: Andy treating historical facts as spoilers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - applying modern entertainment concepts to historical biography

jokes.text: That book sounded so boring, I cried a little.
jokes.setup: Andy's reaction to the Harrison biography
jokes.punchline: Physical reaction to perceived boredom
jokes.explanation: Good Andy emotional overreaction to mundane stimulus

jokes.text: Terry, what's Leslie up to?
jokes.setup: Ron asking Terry for information after saying he won't discuss competition
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting his neutral stance
jokes.explanation: Good setup for recurring gag of Ron's competitive hypocrisy

jokes.text: Toddler cologne. Baby, you smell good.
jokes.setup: Tom pitching toddler cologne investment
jokes.punchline: Slogan suggesting romantic appeal of toddlers
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable joke that relies on inappropriate implications rather than clever writing

jokes.text: Babe, we should sell our dumb house and buy this one.
jokes.setup: Looking at the ruins of Harrison's hunting lodge
jokes.punchline: Andy wanting to buy a pile of old bricks
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - enthusiasm for obviously terrible ideas

jokes.text: There's a bag of old hamburgers over there. What? How old?
jokes.setup: Leslie pointing out literal garbage at the site
jokes.punchline: Andy's interest in the age of discarded hamburgers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - concerned about hamburger freshness at a garbage dump

jokes.text: I found an artifact! Historical artifact! William Henry Harrison's wig.
jokes.setup: Andy finding something at the historical site
jokes.punchline: Obviously not a historical wig from a pile of garbage
jokes.explanation: Predictable Andy enthusiasm for garbage, but well-executed character moment

jokes.text: I signed an oath, Ben. And then a different notary notarized the oath I signed.
jokes.setup: Craig explaining why he can't lie as a notary
jokes.punchline: The recursive bureaucracy of notarizing notary oaths
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic absurdity - the endless loop of official validation

jokes.text: admission to this museum costs $14.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing the pathetic Harrison museum
jokes.punchline: Overcharging for a terrible museum experience
jokes.explanation: Good observation about tourist trap pricing for minimal content

jokes.text: And while you're here, why not visit the Other Famous Harrisons exhibit?
jokes.setup: Museum trying to add value
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to connect to more famous Harrisons
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of museum's desperation for relevance

jokes.text: admission to this museum costs $14.
jokes.setup: Describing this terrible museum
jokes.punchline: They charge real money for this pathetic experience
jokes.explanation: The specificity of $14 makes it funnier - expensive enough to be insulting for such a terrible museum.

jokes.text: Ooh! Like a giant hamster?
jokes.setup: Someone asking what was inside the campaign ball
jokes.punchline: Andy's specific and absurd guess
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy non-sequitur with oddly specific animal choice

jokes.text: I'm intrigued by asymmetrical overalls, angora toothbrushes, and locally sourced Italian flip-flops.
jokes.setup: Annabel describing what's trendy
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd luxury items
jokes.explanation: Good lifestyle blogger parody with escalating product absurdity

jokes.text: Now, try the hottest new craze, beef milk. It's like almond milk that's been squeezed through tiny holes in living cows.
jokes.setup: Annabel introducing a trendy new beverage
jokes.punchline: Reinventing regular milk as artisanal product
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire - rebranding milk as boutique health trend with ridiculous description

jokes.text: That's milk. No. Milk costs $3 a gallon. Annabel's authentic, hand-strained teat-to-table Beef Milk? That costs $60 a gallon.
jokes.setup: Tom pointing out that beef milk is just milk
jokes.punchline: The absurd markup for identical product with fancy branding
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that highlights the absurd economics of lifestyle branding

jokes.text: It's weird and sad and unnecessary.
jokes.setup: April commenting on the Harrison museum
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment delivered as compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - finding the depressing aspects appealing with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Though you do get a yearly stipend of 50,000 Harrison Bucks. Accepted only at that vending machine.
jokes.setup: Museum volunteer compensation
jokes.punchline: Worthless currency only valid at one vending machine
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist economics joke about institutional penny-pinching

jokes.text: The only things I like are dogs, sleeping late, and weird birthmarks.
jokes.setup: April explaining her limited interests
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific third item in the list
jokes.explanation: Perfect April weirdness - escalating from normal to bizarre with total seriousness

jokes.text: No one would ride in the boat with him because he had terrible BO. Then, he went with Janine, our counselor, and he fell asleep and got a boner.
jokes.setup: Andy remembering Zach Harrison from camp
jokes.punchline: Increasingly embarrassing childhood memories
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe humor but relies on basic embarrassment rather than clever writing

jokes.text: So help me God, I will make a mountain out of this molehill.
jokes.setup: Leslie's determination with the weak Harrison evidence
jokes.punchline: Literal reversal of common idiom
jokes.explanation: Clever idiom reversal that perfectly captures Leslie's excessive determination

jokes.text: Calvin Coolidge was a notary. And so was Calvin Coolidge's dad and so was Calvin Coolidge's paternal grandfather.
jokes.setup: Craig sharing notary history trivia
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific Coolidge family notary dynasty
jokes.explanation: Good Craig obsessiveness with specific historical trivia

jokes.text: It's 2.5 pounds of pressure is what you need to get the perfect stamp.
jokes.setup: Continuing notary enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific technical knowledge about stamping
jokes.explanation: Decent character consistency but getting repetitive with notary obsession

jokes.text: Playing with our dog, staring contests, sex with me, etc. And then, I lost the list. So, I had to make a new list. I didn't have any paper, so I wrote it on my leg. And that's when my leg fell asleep.
jokes.setup: Andy made a list to help April find career
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous story about losing and remaking the list
jokes.explanation: Good Andy rambling with sweet intention and absurd execution

jokes.text: And then, I lost the list. So, I had to make a new list. I didn't have any paper, so I wrote it on my leg. And that's when my leg fell asleep.
jokes.setup: Andy making a list for April
jokes.punchline: Cascade of increasingly ridiculous complications
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy rambling story with logical leaps and physical comedy

jokes.text: Could that be possible? Because, you know, I've always thought that we shared very similar jawlines.
jokes.setup: Zach dismissing genealogy as unimportant
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting excited about possible Washington connection
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy - dismissing heritage then getting excited about better heritage

jokes.text: Zach! Camp Wamapoke. You got a boner. We'll catch up. What a dick.
jokes.setup: Zach's introduction as presidential descendant
jokes.punchline: Immediate reminder of embarrassing camp story
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that undermines Leslie's presidential descendant moment

jokes.text: Did he write letters to Thomas Jefferson on his rolltop desk, which, you know, I don't know, might have existed.
jokes.setup: Leslie painting historical picture of Harrison's lodge
jokes.punchline: Admitting mid-speech she's making it up
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment - her enthusiasm overtaking her honesty in real time

jokes.text: Uh... Harrison's a pretty common last name.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking what it's like being Harrison's descendant
jokes.punchline: Zach's deflating response about name commonality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Leslie's manufactured drama with simple logic

jokes.text: 'Wow, that was cool', said some boring idiot from 200 years ago.
jokes.setup: Leslie finishing the historical campaign ball presentation
jokes.punchline: Self-aware dismissal of her own historical recreation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke where Leslie acknowledges the absurdity while doubling down

jokes.text: Fresh. Innovative. Placemaking. Disposable duvets. Growth hacking. Super Moon. Gryzzl.
jokes.setup: Annabel's corporate presentation
jokes.punchline: String of meaningless buzzwords including 'disposable duvets'
jokes.explanation: Solid corporate buzzword satire, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: We had way more quantity than you.
jokes.setup: Defending her presentation against Ron's
jokes.punchline: Leslie proudly claiming victory in quantity over quality
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier strategy with Leslie missing the point

jokes.text: Because I've died somehow and now I'm a ghost living in purgatory until I complete my unfinished business.
jokes.setup: Craig frustrated with the endless form signing
jokes.punchline: Elaborate supernatural explanation for bureaucratic hell
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that captures the eternal nature of bureaucracy

jokes.text: You're not that good at scrapbooking.
jokes.setup: Heated argument between Leslie and Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's oddly specific and mild insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - choosing the most mundane possible insult in a heated moment

jokes.text: That feud is, like, Biggie-Tupac level. Maybe even Morgan Freeman-Shailene Woodley level. Naw. It's bad, but it's not that bad.
jokes.setup: Comparing Leslie and Ron's feud to famous feuds
jokes.punchline: Escalating to random celebrity pairing then backing down
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with absurd celebrity feud reference

jokes.text: Maybe even Morgan Freeman-Shailene Woodley level. Naw. It's bad, but it's not that bad.
jokes.setup: Comparing Ron and Leslie's feud to celebrity feuds
jokes.punchline: Random pairing of Morgan Freeman and Shailene Woodley as ultimate feud
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist pairing that makes no sense but sounds serious

jokes.text: Actually, that might work. Have we tried that?
jokes.setup: Joking about giving them stuffed animals like his triplets
jokes.punchline: Tom seriously considering treating adults like toddlers
jokes.explanation: Good Tom parenting logic applied inappropriately to adults

jokes.text: I'll let you rename the triplets Ruth, Bader, and Ginsburg.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to bribe Leslie to cooperate
jokes.punchline: Supreme Court justice names for babies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben understanding of what would actually motivate Leslie

jokes.text: No!
jokes.setup: Ben trapping them in office together
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous horrified reaction to being locked together
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy moment but fairly predictable sitcom setup
